name: node-istanbul
title: node-istanbul
description:
JavaScript code coverage tool
This package is a JavaScript code coverage tool that computes
statement, line, function and branch coverage with
module loader hooks to transparently add coverage
when running tests.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.

commands.name: istanbul
commands.description: a JS code coverage tool written in JS

arguments.description: coverage against thresholds from coverage JSON files. Exits 1
arguments.raw: -coverage  coverage against thresholds from coverage JSON files. Exits 1

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use istanbul because JavaScript code coverage tool.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use istanbul as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `istanbul help config | <command>` as documented upstream for istanbul.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair istanbul with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `istanbul --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: -coverage
arguments.description: coverage against thresholds from coverage JSON files. Exits 1

documentation.readme: istanbul - a JS code coverage tool written in JS
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install node-istanbul. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `istanbul help config | <command>`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: JavaScript code coverage tool.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: istanbul - a JS code coverage tool written in JS.
